data.title: HSBC Agrees to Sell Singapore Life and Health Insurance Business to Allianz
data.description: According to Jin10, HSBC Holdings said the HSBC Group has agreed to sell HSBC Life Insurance (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. to Allianz for S$2.7 billion, subject to regulatory approval, with completion expected in the first half of 2027. Upon completion, HSBC and Allianz will sign a 15-year exclusive bancassurance distribution agreement, under which HSBC will continue distributing insurance products to its customers in Singapore. HSBC will receive an initial one-off cash payment of S$200 million when the distribution agreement is signed.

data.title: CLO Price Swings Tied to Tokyo, Wharton-Led Study Finds
data.description: The $1.3 trillion U.S. CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street, according to Bloomberg. The Wharton-led study cited in the source says price swings in the market are being dictated by Tokyo alongside U.S. trading.

data.title: Australia's July S&P Global Services PMI Preliminary Reading Rises to 53
data.description: According to Jin10, Australia's July S&P Global Services PMI preliminary reading was 53, compared with a previous reading of 50.5.

data.title: Australia July S&P Global Composite PMI Preliminary Reading Rises to 52.6
data.description: According to Jin10, Australia’s preliminary S&P Global Composite PMI for July was 52.6, compared with 50.4 previously.

data.title: STOCKS | Australia July S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Preliminary Rises to 51.7
data.description: According to Jin10, Australia’s preliminary S&P Global manufacturing PMI for July was 51.7, up from 51.5 previously.

data.title: JetBlue Bonds Sink Ahead of Second-Quarter Earnings
data.description: JetBlue Airways Corp.’s bonds fell ahead of the airline’s second-quarter earnings release next week as investors grew more cautious over mounting economic pressures, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Iran Says It Intercepted a U.S. Tomahawk Missile as Explosions Are Reported in Southern Iran
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran said its air defenses intercepted a U.S. Tomahawk cruise missile over the Kahnuj area of Kerman Province in southeastern Iran on the 23rd. Iran also said two explosions were heard near Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province, and local residents reported multiple explosions in the Strait of Hormuz area south of Larak Island and Hengam Island.

data.title: SEC's Peirce Warns Crypto Vaults And Lending May Trigger Securities Rules
data.description: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce warned on July 22 that moving crypto activity onchain does not remove securities obligations, saying actively managed vaults and lending strategies may require registration. According to BeInCrypto, her “Headstands and Summervaults” statement said vaults can resemble investment contracts, mutual funds, or investment adviser businesses, while onchain lending can also look like a security-style note. Peirce invited vault and lending operators to contact the SEC ahead of her planned departure later this year.

data.title: AI TRENDS | South Korea’s Science Minister Heads to Silicon Valley to Push Tech Investment
data.description:
South Korea’s Science and Technology Minister Bae Kyung-hoon said he planned to leave for Silicon Valley later Thursday evening local time. According to Jin10, he said the trip focuses on strengthening cooperation and encouraging major tech companies to invest in South Korea.

He said the discussions would center on AI cooperation, market expansion, AI data centers, and semiconductors. Bae also said he planned to use the visit to promote South Korea’s AI ecosystem and called the trip a summit aimed at showcasing the strength of "K-AI" to the world.

Bae departed for San Francisco on Thursday evening and met with people related to major U.S. tech companies, including Broadcom, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Nvidia (NVDA.O).

data.title: Stripe Talks to Acquire OpenRouter for Up to $10 Billion
data.description: According to Jin10, Stripe is in talks to acquire OpenRouter. The startup helps developers choose among different AI models. The deal could be announced soon, but the talks could also break down or attract other bidders. The sale valuation may be around $10 billion, and several large technology companies have also previously shown interest in acquiring OpenRouter. Founded in 2023, OpenRouter sells software that lets customers access AI models developed by OpenAI and Anthropic, as well as open-weight models that anyone can download and run.

data.title: GIC Posts Worst Five-Year Return Since 2013; US Remains Top Market
data.description:
Singaporean sovereign wealth fund GIC Pte reported its lowest five-year annualized return in more than a decade after trimming risk earlier and seeing bond holdings fail to fully rebound, according to Bloomberg.

The US remained GIC’s top market.

data.title: STOCKS | Lyntris Files for US IPO Backed by Trive Capital
data.description: Lyntris Inc., a defense technology firm backed by Trive Capital, has filed for a US initial public offering, according to Bloomberg. The filing comes as defense companies continue to rush toward the public markets.

data.title: STOCKS | Yen Slide Threatens Japan Stock Rally
data.description:
The yen’s relentless decline is raising concern it could eventually curb the foreign inflows that have helped push Japanese equities to record highs, according to Bloomberg.

Bloomberg Economics said the currency weakness poses a new risk to the bull run in Japan stocks.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Rise by 1.427 Tons
data.description: According to Jin10, global gold ETF SPDR Gold Trust increased its holdings by 1.427 tons from the previous day to 1,009.298 tons.

data.title: AI | Genesis AI in Talks to Raise About $500 Million
data.description:
Genesis AI is in talks with investors to raise around $500 million in a new funding round, according to people familiar with the efforts and Bloomberg reported.

The AI robotics startup did not disclose further details on the proposed financing.

data.title: Strategy Overhauls MSTR Metrics To Show Bitcoin Owed To Lenders First
data.description: Strategy, formerly MicroStrategy, overhauled its MSTR metrics to show how much of its Bitcoin pile is owed to lenders and preferred investors before common shareholders, according to BeInCrypto, as founder and executive chairman Michael Saylor said Bitcoin capital markets require a new financial language. The company says it holds about 843,775 Bitcoin, with about $22 billion owed first, leaving roughly $36 billion as net reserve for common shareholders; it also says servicing debt and preferred stock costs about $1.8 billion a year.

data.title: STOCKS | Fed Seen Keeping Rates Unchanged in July at 65.3%
data.description: According to Jin10, CME FedWatch shows a 65.3% probability that the Federal Reserve will keep rates unchanged in July, with a 34.7% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point hike. The probability that the Federal Reserve will keep rates unchanged through September is 17.6%, while the probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point hike is 57% and the probability of a cumulative 50-basis-point hike is 25.4%.

data.title: Dish Cleared to Poll Creditors on Restructuring Plan
data.description:
Dish Network won court approval to poll creditors on a restructuring deal that would cut billions of dollars of debt from its satellite-TV business and wind down its failed 5G buildout, according to Bloomberg.

The ruling lets Dish seek creditor support for the plan as it works through its restructuring process.

data.title: Advent Hires Morgan Stanley to Sell Stake in Brazil’s Tigre
data.description:
Advent hired Morgan Stanley to explore a potential sale of its stake in Tigre SA, a Brazilian manufacturer of pipe and hydraulic systems, according to Bloomberg.

People familiar with the matter said the firm is weighing options for the holding, but no transaction has been announced.

data.title: Eric Balchunas Says HedgeEye Launches Democratic and Republican Prediction ETFs
data.description: Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said on X that HedgeEye has joined the prediction market ETF race by launching Democratic and Republican prediction ETFs. According to Odaily, each ETF will track the election performance of its party in U.S. federal elections.
